evals.prompt: Audit this unfamiliar Flask backend and, if you find worthwhile issues, refactor it toward contextual MVC while preserving its HTTP behavior. Target root: fixture. Save the audit report to outputs/audit.md. I need exact file-and-line evidence. Use your judgment and complete as much as possible without waiting for further clarification.
evals.expected_output: A Flask e-commerce fingerprint and evidence-backed ordered audit written to outputs/audit.md, followed by an explicit approval request tied to the reviewed report; the fixture remains unchanged.
expectations: Identifies Python, Flask, the e-commerce domain, app.py as an entry point, SQLite persistence, and the current responsibility shape.
Records boot, endpoint, representative domain-flow, persistence, and discovery limitations before making architecture judgments.
Writes outputs/audit.md with fingerprint, scope, baseline, limitations, severity summary in CRITICAL-HIGH-MEDIUM-LOW order, findings, proposed scope, approval prompt, and snapshot digest.
Contains at least five root-cause-distinct findings, including at least one CRITICAL or HIGH finding.
Every finding uses an existing repository-relative file and valid one-based inclusive line range with non-empty evidence, impact, and recommendation.
Any deprecated-API claim is tied to the detected dependency version and authoritative current documentation; otherwise deprecation uncertainty is recorded as a limitation.
Does not mutate fixture source, configuration, dependencies, schemas, databases, or tests before explicit approval tied to the reviewed report.
Proposes responsibility-based MVC changes and names boot, endpoint, domain-flow, persistence, and security validation obligations without forcing one directory template.

evals.prompt: Review this legacy Express API for architecture, security, data-integrity, performance, and deprecated-API problems, then modernize it toward responsibility-based MVC if appropriate. Target root: fixture. Write the audit to outputs/audit.md. Preserve endpoint meaning and checkout behavior. Continue independently unless a truly mandatory decision blocks you.
evals.expected_output: An Express LMS fingerprint and root-cause-distinct ordered audit written to outputs/audit.md, followed by a report-specific approval gate before any fixture mutation.
expectations: Identifies JavaScript, Express, the LMS domain, src/app.js as the entry point, SQLite persistence, and AppManager-centered responsibilities.
Records boot, endpoint, checkout/report/deletion flows, persistence, and discovery limitations before making architecture judgments.
Writes outputs/audit.md with fingerprint, scope, baseline, limitations, severity summary in CRITICAL-HIGH-MEDIUM-LOW order, findings, proposed scope, approval prompt, and snapshot digest.
Contains at least five root-cause-distinct findings, including at least one CRITICAL or HIGH finding.
Every finding uses an existing repository-relative file and valid one-based inclusive line range with non-empty evidence, impact, and recommendation.
Any deprecated-API claim is tied to the detected dependency version and authoritative current documentation; otherwise deprecation uncertainty is recorded as a limitation.
Does not mutate fixture source, configuration, dependencies, schemas, databases, or tests before explicit approval tied to the reviewed report.
Proposes contextual decomposition of AppManager plus boot, endpoint, checkout, persistence, rollback, and security validation without imposing Flask conventions.

evals.prompt: Audit and improve this partially layered Flask task-management API. Target root: fixture. Save the audit to outputs/audit.md. Retain useful models, blueprints, services, and helpers; change only responsibilities that evidence shows are misplaced. Preserve routes, status semantics, response meaning, and persistence effects. Please carry the work through as far as safely possible.
evals.expected_output: A Flask-SQLAlchemy task-management fingerprint and contextual ordered audit written to outputs/audit.md, followed by an immutable approval gate with no fixture mutation.
expectations: Identifies Python, Flask-SQLAlchemy, the task-management domain, app.py as an entry point, SQLite persistence, and the existing layered shape.
Records boot, endpoint, task-user-category flows, persistence, and discovery limitations before making architecture judgments.
Writes outputs/audit.md with fingerprint, scope, baseline, limitations, severity summary in CRITICAL-HIGH-MEDIUM-LOW order, findings, proposed scope, approval prompt, and snapshot digest.
Contains at least five root-cause-distinct findings, including at least one CRITICAL or HIGH finding.
Every finding uses an existing repository-relative file and valid one-based inclusive line range with non-empty evidence, impact, and recommendation.
Any deprecated-API claim is tied to the detected dependency version and authoritative current documentation; otherwise deprecation uncertainty is recorded as a limitation.
Does not mutate fixture source, configuration, dependencies, schemas, databases, or tests before explicit approval tied to the reviewed report.
Retains useful models, blueprints, services, and helpers while proposing targeted responsibility corrections and complete behavioral validation.
